content.thinking: The user wants me to run a workflow using the Workflow tool with a specific script path. Let me do that.

description: cc-review brain: token-optimized, deterministic, zero-tolerance PR/MR review as one self-contained workflow. A single opus-medium TRIAGE pass posts a pre-review walkthrough (mermaid + difficulty 1-5) and parses the @cc-review intent; the difficulty rating deterministically scales the review path (reviewer count, opus effort high/xhigh, recon/grade/verify on/off). 1-2 opus reviewers (top-relevant domains, xhigh only for difficulty 4-5) anchor a difficulty-scaled sonnet squad. All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

summary: cc-review brain: token-optimized, deterministic, zero-tolerance PR/MR review as one self-contained workflow. A single opus-medium TRIAGE pass posts a pre-review walkthrough (mermaid + difficulty 1-5) and parses the @cc-review intent; the difficulty rating deterministically scales the review path (reviewer count, opus effort high/xhigh, recon/grade/verify on/off). 1-2 opus reviewers (top-relevant domains, xhigh only for difficulty 4-5) anchor a difficulty-scaled sonnet squad. All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

A single opus-medium TRIAGE pass posts a pre-review walkthrough (mermaid + difficulty 1-5) and parses the @cc-review intent; the difficulty rating deterministically scales the review path (reviewer count, opus effort high/xhigh, recon/grade/verify on/off). 1-2 opus reviewers (top-relevant domains, xhigh only for difficulty 4-5) anchor a difficulty-scaled sonnet squad. All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

workflow_progress.label: post-overview
workflow_progress.promptPreview:
Post the pre-review walkthrough comment. Write the markdown below VERBATIM to /tmp/cc-overview.md using bash (a cat heredoc — NOT the Write tool; /tmp is outside the agent's writable workspace), then run:
  tsx ${CC_REVIEW_BOT_DIR:-.}/scripts/review-platform.ts post-overview --pr 12 --body-file /tmp/cc-overview.md
Report the comment id. Markdown:
<<<MD

workflow_progress.label: tools
workflow_progress.promptPreview:
Run the analysis tool-runner from the repo root and return its JSON verbatim:
  tsx ${CC_REVIEW_BOT_DIR:-.}/scripts/analysis-tools.ts --changed
It reports which tools were available and any findings. If the script is missing or no tools are installed, return {"available":[],"findings":[]}.

summary: cc-review brain: token-optimized, deterministic, zero-tolerance PR/MR review as one self-contained workflow. A single opus-medium TRIAGE pass posts a pre-review walkthrough (mermaid + difficulty 1-5) and parses the @cc-review intent; the difficulty rating deterministically scales the review path (reviewer count, opus effort high/xhigh, recon/grade/verify on/off). 1-2 opus reviewers (top-relevant domains, xhigh only for difficulty 4-5) anchor a difficulty-scaled sonnet squad. All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

All platform I/O routes through scripts/review-platform.ts (GitHub bot / GitLab local). Posting gated on args.post===true.

69	    "overview": "## feats CLI and runner test coverage — Difficulty: 2/5\n\nThis PR adds end-to-end and unit test coverage for the `feats` BDD runner with no production code changes. It exercises three layers: `parseArgs`/`runFeatsCli` (argument parsing, exit codes, meta flags, glob resolution, tag filtering), `runCore` (structured summary, reporter event ordering, lifecycle hooks, world factory), and the newly exported `runScenarioPure` (background steps, Before/After hook ordering, tag-filtered hooks, step skipping after failure). The CLI tests scaffold temp projects on disk, monkeypatch `process.stdout/stderr.write` to capture output, and assert on exit codes and emitted text.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    A[parseArgs argv] --> B{flags valid?}\n    B -- no --> C[throw CliError exit=2]\n    B -- yes --> D[runFeatsCli]\n    D --> E[resolveRequires glob steps]\n    D --> F[loadFeatures glob]\n    E --> G[runCore features]\n    F --> G\n    G --> H[runScenarioPure per scenario]\n    H --> I[reporters: onStart..onStep..onEnd]\n    G --> J[summary + exitCode]\n    J --> K[process exit]\n```\n\nRiskiest area to watch: the global `process.stdout/stderr.write` reassignment shared across many tests — if `runFeatsCli` throws before the `finally` restore in any helper, or if bun:test runs these concurrently, capture state can leak between tests and produce flaky or cross-contaminated output assertions.",
